faults.use_case: 1.1 Taxi: Submit order / 1.5 Taxi: Receive order
faults.description: Confirm signal absent from Taxi-to-Driver API (3.2.2). Both UC 1.1 Task 4 and UC 1.5 Task 4 require the central to confirm an order to the driver, but the taxi module has no Confirm signal to forward this confirmation from the central to the driver.

No signal from Central to Taxi when no cars are available at the requested time. Variant 4b requires the driver to be informed so a new order with a different time can be submitted, but no such notification signal is defined in the Central-to-Taxi API.

Order_struct has no priority field. Task 2 states that orders can be prioritized, with some customers being more important than others, but the data structure defines no attribute to carry this priority information.

No signal defined for the system to send a time estimate to the operator when an order is very close in time. Variant 3b requires this estimate, but no corresponding signal exists in the Central-to-Operator API.
LoginSignal is defined without parameters. Task 3 requires the terminal to send driver identification information to the central, but LoginSignal carries no driver ID data.
LoggedIn(integer) carries only the taxi number. Task 3 requires driver information to be sent to the central upon login, but no driver identification parameter is present in the LoggedIn signal.

faults.use_case: 1.4 Taxi: Log in / 1.9 Taxi: View traffic overview
faults.description: ZoneInfo signal is absent from Central-to-Taxi API. Task 6 of UC 1.4 requires the central to proactively send zone overview after login; Task 2 of UC 1.9 requires it in response to an Overview request. Neither can be fulfilled without ZoneInfo in 3.4.1.

LoginFailed signal absent from Central-to-Taxi API. Variant 4b requires the central to reject an invalid card and leave the car in Offline state, but no LoginFailed signal exists in 3.4.1 to communicate the rejection to the taxi module.

No signal from Central to Taxi for automatic order cancellation after the 2-minute timeout. Variant 3b requires the order to be automatically canceled, but the design defines no cancellation notification from Central to Taxi.

faults.use_case: 1.5 Taxi: Receive order / 1.6 Taxi: Driving a customer
faults.description: No signal to communicate the Waiting-for-Customer state to the central. After accepting an order (UC 1.5 Task 6), the car transitions to Waiting-for-Customer before meter start, but no signal for this state change is defined.

Position(integer, integer) is missing a time parameter. Task 8 requires the driver to send both arrival zone and expected arrival time to the central, but Position only carries taxi number and zone number — no time field.

faults.use_case: 1.7 Central: Handle incoming alarms / 1.8 Taxi: Alarm event
faults.description: No alarm trigger signal defined in Taxi-to-Central API. UC 1.8 Tasks 1-2 require the taxi to alert the central when the driver activates the alarm and to open a voice channel, but 3.4.2 defines no alarm notification signal.

Position signal sends zone number, not exact coordinates. Task 3 requires the taxi to transmit exact coordinates to the central every 30 seconds during an alarm, but Position(integer, integer) only provides a coarse zone identifier.

MSC 4.1 is missing a Central-to-Taxi Confirm after step 8 (Ack received by central). UC 1.5 Task 4 requires the central to confirm the accepted order back to the driver, but no such step is shown in the MSC.

faults.use_case: 1.1 Taxi: Submit order / 1.5 Taxi: Receive order
faults.description: Confirm signal missing from the communication protocol table (Table 1). Confirm is sent from Central to Taxi over the radio link (3.4.1), so it must be defined in the protocol, but no Confirm message type is present in Table 1.

No signal to notify the operator when a designated car rejects a manually dispatched order. Variant 4b requires the operator to be informed so the order can be reassigned, but no rejection notification is defined in 3.3.2.
No text message acknowledgment signal from Taxi to Central. Task 4 requires each receiving car to automatically acknowledge receipt to the central, but no acknowledgment signal is defined in 3.4.2.
No signal to notify the operator of failed text message delivery to a specific car. Variant 4b requires this notification, but no corresponding signal is defined in the Central-to-Operator API.

Text_Message signal has no group mechanism. Task 3 requires the operator to be able to send to a specific car, a group of cars, or all cars, but the signal only supports one specific car (by number) or all cars (parameter 0) — no group is definable.

faults.use_case: 1.12 Central: Log in to central system
faults.description: No operator login signal defined in Central-Operator API. UC 1.12 requires operators to authenticate via user identification and password, but 3.3.1 contains no signal for submitting login credentials.
Taxi(integer, integer, integer) is placed in 3.3.1 (Operator-to-Central signals) but its description reads 'this is the response', indicating it should be in 3.3.2 (Central-to-Operator signals).

Confirm_Voice missing from User-to-Taxi signals (3.2.1). MSC 4.2 step 5 shows the driver sending Confirm_Voice to the taxi module to confirm a voice connection, but this signal is not defined in 3.2.1.

No signal for the driver to request car and driver properties (3.2.1) and no signal from Central to Taxi to deliver the requested information (3.4.1). Both a request and response signal are required by UC 1.16 but neither is defined.
No signal for updating driver properties in the Central-Operator API. UC 1.18 requires the operator to select a driver and submit updated properties, but 3.3.1 defines no such signal.
